skill.name: gh-issue-fix-flow
skill.description: Guide an agent through a GitHub issue fix from issue intake to code change, validation, commit, and push.
skill.summary: Resolve GitHub issues with a structured inspect, edit, test, commit, and push workflow.
skill.author: Dimillian

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found inline command examples and a weak cryptography alert. The weak cryptography alert is a false positive because SKILL.md line 3 is descriptive text, not cryptographic code. The external command detections are Markdown examples, but the skill intentionally guides agents to run gh, rg, sed, XcodeBuildMCP, and git push, so it has legitimate but elevated operational risk.

medium_findings.title: External Command and Repository Mutation Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to inspect GitHub issues, search files, run builds or tests, commit changes, and push to a remote repository. This is appropriate for the stated coding workflow, but it can modify source code and publish commits if used with repository credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe command-driven issue handling and git push. The intent is legitimate, but the workflow can mutate a repository, so a medium risk rating is warranted.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Commands Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the evidence is Markdown inline command text in SKILL.md. There is no executable Ruby code in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown skill document and the flagged text is enclosed as inline documentation. No code interpreter or Ruby execution context is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3 as weak cryptography, but that line is a YAML description of an end-to-end GitHub workflow. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only natural language metadata. I found no evidence of cryptographic code in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Push Included in Agent Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells agents to commit and push changes. This is expected for the workflow, but it should be used only in trusted repositories with reviewed diffs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The push step is explicitly documented. It is not malicious by itself, but it can publish repository changes.

content.user_title: Fix GitHub Issues End to End
content.value_statement: GitHub issue fixes often lose context between triage, coding, validation, and release steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for inspecting an issue, changing code, testing, committing, and pushing.
actual_capabilities: Guides issue intake with GitHub CLI commands and comment review.
Directs code path discovery with repository search tools.
Promotes minimal code edits aligned with local project style.
Includes build and test validation through XcodeBuildMCP.
Defines commit and push steps with issue-closing messages.
Requires reporting changed files, test results, and follow-up items.
limitations: Requires a configured GitHub CLI and repository access.
Assumes the project can use XcodeBuildMCP for relevant builds or tests.
Does not decide product requirements when an issue is ambiguous.
Can publish changes if the agent has git push permission.

use_cases.title: Resolve a Maintained App Bug
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to read an issue, locate the failing code path, implement a focused fix, test it, and push a closing commit.

use_cases.title: Standardize Contributor Fixes
use_cases.description: Give contributors a repeatable process for issue context, local validation, clean commits, and transparent handoff notes.

use_cases.title: Drive Agentic Maintenance Work
use_cases.description: Use the skill as a checklist for an AI coding agent that must complete issue work without skipping validation or status reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Fix One Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single issue in the current repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-issue-fix-flow to fix issue #123 in this repository. Inspect the issue, make the smallest correct change, run relevant tests, commit with a closing message, and push.

prompt_templates.title: Fix With Repository Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Issue work when the owner and repository must be explicit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-issue-fix-flow for issue #123 in owner/repo. Confirm the issue context with comments, find the relevant code path, implement the fix, validate it, and report the result.

prompt_templates.title: Fix and Preserve Local Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow where unrelated local changes may exist.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-issue-fix-flow to fix issue #123. Check for unrelated changes before editing, avoid staging files outside the fix, run targeted validation, and summarize any remaining risks.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Verified Issue Closure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow requiring careful validation and publication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-issue-fix-flow to resolve issue #123 in owner/repo. Capture reproduction details, follow project instructions, add tests if practical, run XcodeBuildMCP validation, commit with a closes message, push, and provide a concise completion report.

output_examples.input: Fix issue #42 in the current repository.
output_examples.output: The agent reports the issue summary, changed files, validation command results, commit message, push status, and any follow-up risks.

output_examples.input: Fix issue #18, but do not include unrelated local changes.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies unrelated files, leaves them unstaged, commits only the issue fix, and notes the excluded files in the final report.

output_examples.input: Resolve a failing macOS issue with simulator fallback if needed.
output_examples.output: The agent runs the preferred build or test path, falls back to simulator validation when appropriate, and reports both the failure reason and final result.

best_practices: Run the workflow only in repositories where the agent may inspect issues and push commits.
Review `git status --short` and the final diff before allowing a commit or push.
Prefer targeted tests first, then broader validation when the change affects shared behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for repositories where the agent lacks permission to push.
Do not skip issue comments, because maintainers may add important constraints there.
Do not stage unrelated local changes while preparing the issue fix commit.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow instructions for an AI agent. The agent or environment must have the required tools and permissions.

faq.question: Which tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects GitHub CLI, repository search tools such as rg, git, and XcodeBuildMCP for Apple project builds or tests.

faq.question: Can it work outside Xcode projects?
faq.answer: The issue triage and git workflow can apply elsewhere, but the build and test section is written for XcodeBuildMCP.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can guide agents to run commands, edit files, commit, and push. That is useful, but it requires trusted repository access.

faq.question: Does it handle ambiguous issues?
faq.answer: It tells the agent to capture context and maintainer notes. It does not replace human product decisions when requirements are unclear.

faq.question: How should teams review its output?
faq.answer: Teams should inspect the diff, confirm test results, and verify that only intended files were staged and pushed.
